payload.subject: HI construct validity finding + adversarial register (AR) dimension proposal
payload.summary: Turn 7 flagged the HI direction anomaly (hostile_social_media_anchor HI=6.88 > policy_brief HI=6.15) as a possible calibration artifact. It is not. Diagnosis: HI measures narrator-experienced hostility, not authorial adversarial intent. This is a construct×distribution mismatch that makes HI invalid as a content-type classifier. A replacement measure (adversarial register, AR) has been designed, grounded, rubric-built, and Phase 1 validated on the same 5-text corpus. PSQ-Lite revision recommended.

hi_construct_finding.anomaly: hostile_social_media_anchor HI=6.88 (safer on 0–10 scale) vs. policy_brief HI=6.15 (less safe). Expected ordering was reversed.
hi_construct_finding.root_cause: Construct×distribution mismatch. PSQ was trained on Dreaddit (Reddit stress posts — narrator-centric corpus). HI measures hostility as experienced by the narrator — overt aggression, passive undermining, or structural antagonism directed at the narrator (psq-definition.md §5). It does not measure authorial adversarial intent.

mechanism.hostile_anchor: Author is being hostile outward (UN, foreign bureaucrats). Narrator is not a victim — they are the hostile actor. PSQ reads this as the narrator facing relatively low incoming hostility. HI scores high (safer).
mechanism.policy_brief: Describes institutional inertia from the narrator's perspective. 'Committee has not held a hearing since Clinton administration' = structural antagonism that the narrator (advocate) faces. PSQ reads this as structural hostility toward the narrator. HI scores lower (less safe).

hi_construct_finding.verdict: HI is operating correctly given its training distribution. The anomaly is not a model error — it is a construct mismatch between what HI measures and what content-type classification requires.
hi_construct_finding.implication_for_psq_lite: The current PSQ-Lite formula (TE + HI(raw) + TC) is invalid for content-type discrimination. HI is measuring narrator vulnerability to hostility, not the adversarial register of the content itself. A text can score high HI (low narrator-experienced hostility) precisely because its author is the hostile agent — which inverts the expected classification signal.
hi_construct_finding.calibration_note: Raw model scores show the same ordering as calibrated scores. This is not fixable with recalibration. The fix requires a different dimension.

adversarial_register_proposal.construct: Measures the degree to which a text's rhetorical mode is oriented toward defeating, discrediting, or excluding an opposing position — as opposed to informing, advocating, or deliberating. Scores what the text does, not what the author meant.
adversarial_register_proposal.key_property: Observable text property. Does not require inference about author intent. Grounded in three independent bodies of literature.

theoretical_grounding.contribution: Eristic dialogue (winning at all costs) anchors the adversarial end; deliberative dialogue anchors the cooperative end. Provides the primary classification spine.
Decomposes stance into evaluation of object, self-positioning, and alignment/disalignment invitation. AR maximizes all three adversarial dimensions simultaneously.
Hostile attribution (ascribing malicious or illegitimate motives to opponents) is the single strongest discriminating feature of eristic register.

scoring_dimensions.bands: 0-2 eristic / 3-4 adversarial persuasion / 5-6 informational / 7-8 advocacy / 9-10 deliberative
0-2 hostile / 3-4 competence attack / 5-6 neutral / 7-8 structurally charitable / 9-10 explicitly charitable

adversarial_register_proposal.scale: 0–10. PSQ convention: 0 = maximum adversarial register (eristic); 10 = minimum adversarial register (deliberative).

phase_1_validation.corpus: 5-text ICESCR corpus (same texts as psq-scoring session)

validation_criteria.inter_rater: N/A — single session; rubric available for independent replication

gap_signal_table.interpretation: Large positive: adversarial author, not threatening narrator environment
Near-zero: adversarial register and psychoemotional environment aligned
Slight negative: advocacy generates mild environmental exposure despite non-adversarial intent
Moderate positive: factual framing reads safer than implicit 'fifty years of inaction' charge

psq_lite_recommendation.problem: HI is invalid for content-type classification (measures narrator vulnerability, not authorial adversarial register).
psq_lite_recommendation.proposed_revision: TE + TC + AR — replaces HI with AR for advocacy content classification
psq_lite_recommendation.rationale: AR correctly orders the 5 texts by adversarial register. HI inverts the expected classification signal for this corpus. TC and TE remain valid.
psq_lite_recommendation.status: Proposal — PSQ-Lite adoption decision belongs to unratified-agent. AR is currently LLM heuristic only (Phase 1). Phase 2 (label generation) and Phase 3 (DistilBERT head) required before AR can be served from the production endpoint.
psq_lite_recommendation.interim_option: Apply AR scoring prompt (docs/adversarial-register-rubric.md §Scoring Prompt) as a Claude Code heuristic until Phase 3 deployment. Consistent with PSQ-Lite's existing LLM heuristic approach.

integration_path.phase_1: COMPLETE — LLM heuristic validated on 5-text corpus
integration_path.phase_2: Label generation via label_separated.py on psq.db training corpus. One session.
integration_path.phase_3: Add as 11th DistilBERT dimension head in distill.py. Retrain.
integration_path.phase_4: AR available from production endpoint. Revise PSQ-Lite formula to TE + TC + AR.

claims.text: HI direction anomaly is a construct×distribution mismatch, not a calibration error. Raw model scores show the same inverted ordering as calibrated scores.
claims.confidence_basis: Raw scores from calibration.json vs. psq-definition.md §5 construct definition. Two independent confirming signals: (1) psq-definition.md explicitly includes 'structural antagonism' as HI sub-type, explaining policy_brief; (2) raw scores confirm this is not a calibration artifact.

claims.text: Adversarial register rubric passes all three checkable Phase 1 validation criteria on the 5-text ICESCR corpus.
claims.confidence_basis: Single scoring session by psychology-agent. Discrimination check, ordering check, and gap signal check all pass. Inter-rater reliability requires a second independent session.

claims.text: HI is invalid as a content-type classifier for advocacy content. The current PSQ-Lite formula (TE + HI(raw) + TC) should not be relied on for this purpose.
claims.confidence_basis: Follows directly from construct×distribution mismatch finding. Confidence 0.85 not 1.0 because there may be corpus-specific recovery patterns not yet examined.

action_gate.gate_condition: Recommendation to revise PSQ-Lite formula — unratified-agent decision
action_gate.gate_note: No blocking action required. Advisory message. unratified-agent may: (a) continue using HI(raw) in PSQ-Lite with explicit caveat that it measures narrator vulnerability not authorial adversarial register, (b) adopt AR heuristic via scoring prompt for interim classification, or (c) defer until Phase 3 endpoint deployment. Response not required.

epistemic_flags: HI construct finding based on single corpus (5 ICESCR advocacy texts) — not validated on held-out data
AR Phase 1 validation: single scoring session only; inter-rater reliability check pending
PSQ-Lite revision recommendation is provisional — depends on AR Phase 2/3 completion timeline
